learningModes.title: Worked example + fading
learningModes.description: See a complete derivation, then take back one step at a time.
learningModes.useWhen: You cannot begin or lose procedural steps.

learningModes.title: Predict → reveal
learningModes.description: Predict what changes before the whiteboard reveals it.
learningModes.useWhen: You make wrong predictions about changing conditions.

learningModes.title: Self-explanation
learningModes.description: Explain why each transformation is valid.
learningModes.useWhen: You get the answer but cannot explain why.

learningModes.title: Retrieval practice
learningModes.description: Recall a rule or equation before seeing notes.
learningModes.useWhen: You cannot recall definitions, equations, or rules.

learningModes.title: Spaced review
learningModes.description: Return to a concept at increasing intervals.
learningModes.useWhen: You understand today but forget later.

learningModes.title: Interleaved contrast
learningModes.description: Compare nearby concepts in mixed practice.

learningModes.title: Concrete example
learningModes.description: Connect the abstraction to a real signal or measurement.
learningModes.useWhen: You know the formula but not when to use it.

learningModes.title: Representation switch
learningModes.description: Move between equation, waveform, table, diagram, and prose.
learningModes.useWhen: You understand one representation but not another.

learningModes.title: Exam bridge
learningModes.description: Apply the concept to one bounded exam-style problem.
learningModes.useWhen: You understand the idea but cannot apply it under exam conditions.

modules.title: Sampling and aliasing
modules.objective: Predict how a continuous cosine appears after sampling and explain when two frequencies become indistinguishable.

checkpoints.prompt: If the sampling rate increases while the signal stays the same, what happens to the distance between sample points?
For a 10 Hz sinusoid, which sampling rate avoids aliasing under the Nyquist condition?
At Fs = 40 Hz, do 10 Hz and 50 Hz produce the same samples? Explain why or why not.
Change Fs below the Nyquist rate and explain the visible waveform change.

examBridge.prompt: Given x(t)=cos(2π·50t) sampled at Fs=40 Hz, identify the equivalent discrete-time frequency and justify it.
